summary: Strong agent-readiness with 0 high-priority issue(s) remaining.
recommendations: Use opt-in command probes in trusted environments to verify setup and tests.

scoringCaps.reason: Less than 70% of applicable checks were evaluated.
One or more key agent-readiness artifacts are shallow or partial.

findings.title: OpenAPI file detected
findings.description: The scanner found an API schema that agents can use.
findings.recommendation: Keep API descriptions, examples, and auth details current.

findings.title: MCP readiness could not be evaluated yet
findings.description: No MCP server/config/package signal was detected.
evidence: No file or package name matching MCP was scanned.
findings.recommendation: If this project exposes MCP tools, include server files and clear tool descriptions.

findings.title: Command verification probes were skipped
findings.description: Static analysis is default; project commands are not executed unless explicitly enabled.
evidence: Run with --probe commands or --run-probes to execute safe script probes.
findings.recommendation: Use command probes in trusted local or CI environments when you want executable verification.
findings.agentFailureMode: Without command probes, AgentLighthouse can tell agents what commands appear to exist, but not whether they currently pass.

humanReadableProjectSignals.summary: Human-readable project signals describe conventional repo, docs, examples, and API context.

agentSpecificContextLayer.summary: Agent-specific context is the machine-readable layer that helps coding agents work safely.

verifiability.summary: Verifiability measures whether setup, tests, examples, and workflows can be checked.

notes: Artifact exists but no text content was available for quality checks.

recommendedActions: Use opt-in command probes in trusted environments to verify setup and tests.

Sample Good Project is a small TypeScript API service for AgentLighthouse validation.

## Development Workflow

- Install with `pnpm install`.
- Run `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm test` before handing work back.

textByPath.AGENTS.md:
# Agent Instructions for Sample Good Project

## Project Overview

Sample Good Project is a TypeScript API service used to validate agent-readiness checks.

## Setup Commands

```bash
pnpm install
```

```bash
pnpm dev
```

```bash
pnpm build
```

## Test, Lint, and Typecheck Commands

```bash
pnpm test
```

```bash
pnpm lint
```

```bash
pnpm typecheck
```

## Architecture Map

- `src/` contains application code.
- `docs/` contains architecture and API documentation.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` contains agent task workflows.

## Coding Conventions

- Use strict TypeScript.
- Keep functions small and named for their behavior.
- Prefer deterministic logic and explicit error handling.

## Common Mistakes

- Do not edit generated `dist/`, `coverage/`, or build output.
- Avoid committing local environment files.

## Privacy and Security

- Never commit secrets, credentials, tokens, or private data.
- Use placeholders such as `EXAMPLE_API_KEY`.
- Do not send code or docs to external LLMs without user approval.

## Task Workflows

- Add a small endpoint.
- Run lint, typecheck, and tests.
- Update docs and benchmarks when behavior changes.

## Ownership

Maintainers review API contract changes, benchmark workflow changes, and any security-sensitive examples.

textByPath.README.md:
# Sample Good Project

Sample Good Project is a small TypeScript API service used to validate AgentLighthouse scoring on a well-documented repository.

## Quickstart

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```

## Installation

Use pnpm with Node.js 22 or newer.

```bash
pnpm install
```

## Usage Example

```bash
pnpm test
```

The example API exposes a health helper in `src/index.ts`.

## Architecture

- `src/` contains TypeScript source code.
- `docs/` contains architecture and API guidance.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` contains realistic agent task workflows.

## Development Commands

```bash
pnpm lint
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test
pnpm build
```

## Troubleshooting

If commands fail, verify Node.js 22+, run `pnpm install`, and confirm `EXAMPLE_API_KEY` is used instead of real credentials.
textByPath.agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml:
version: 0.1.0
project: sample-good-project
tasks:
  - id: run-quality-gates
    title: Run quality gates
    persona: maintainer using an AI coding agent
    objective: Install dependencies, then run lint, typecheck, and tests using the documented package manager and report failures with actionable file paths.
    projectAreas:
      - setup
      - tests
      - ci
    requiredDocs:
      - README.md
      - AGENTS.md
      - docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
    allowedFiles:
      - package.json
      - src/**
      - docs/**
    disallowedFiles:
      - node_modules/**
      - dist/**
    expectedActions:
      - Identify pnpm as the package manager.
      - Run lint, typecheck, and tests.
    expectedOutputs:
      - Command summary with pass/fail status and file paths for failures.
    successCriteria:
      - The agent identifies pnpm as the package manager.
      - The agent runs the documented commands.
      - The agent reports failures with file paths.
    verificationCommands:
      - pnpm lint
      - pnpm typecheck
      - pnpm test
    riskLevel: low
    commonFailureModes:
      - Uses npm instead of pnpm.
      - Reports success without running all quality gates.
  - id: find-architecture
    title: Find architecture map
    persona: new contributor using an AI coding agent
    objective: Explain where source code, docs, OpenAPI specs, and benchmark tasks live using only documented project context.
    projectAreas:
      - docs
      - repo-structure
    requiredDocs:
      - README.md
      - AGENTS.md
      - docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
    allowedFiles:
      - README.md
      - AGENTS.md
      - docs/**
      - agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml
    disallowedFiles:
      - node_modules/**
    expectedActions:
      - Read README and architecture docs.
      - Cross-check the benchmark file location.
    expectedOutputs:
      - Architecture summary with file paths.
    successCriteria:
      - The agent references README, AGENTS.md, and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
      - The explanation is consistent with the repository structure.
    verificationCommands:
      - pnpm test
    riskLevel: low
    commonFailureModes:
      - Invents directories not present in the repo.
      - Ignores agent-specific instructions.
textByPath.docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:
# Architecture

Sample Good Project keeps source code in `src/`, documentation in `docs/`, and agent task workflows in `benchmarks/`.

The main workflow is:

```text
install -> lint -> typecheck -> test -> build
```

textByPath.openapi.yaml:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Sample Good Project API
  version: 0.1.0
  description: Sample Good Project API for health checks and widget workflows with clear sandbox authentication, examples, error responses, and retry guidance for generated clients.
servers:
  - url: https://api.sample-good.example/v1
security:
  - bearerAuth: []
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      operationId: getServiceHealth
      summary: Check service health
      description: Returns a simple health payload that agents can use to verify the API is reachable during local development. This endpoint does not mutate data and may be retried after 429 Retry-After responses.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Health response
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                ok: true
        "401":
          description: Missing or invalid bearer token.
        "429":
          description: Too many requests; retry after the Retry-After header.
